theme.title: Object Pronouns in Context
theme.description: B1 practice with direct and indirect object pronouns, contrast, and more natural sentence patterns.

lesson.title: Lesson 10: B1 Review
lesson.instructions: Review direct and indirect objects in mixed realistic situations with more natural B1 phrasing.

exercises.prompt: Did you tell your coworkers the news? No, I still have not told it to them.
exercises.answer: Les contaste la noticia a tus companeros de trabajo? No, todavia no se la he contado.
exercises.explanation: A classic review item with 'se la'.

exercises.prompt: Lo habia preparado para ellos, pero al final no quisieron probarlo.
exercises.answer: I had prepared it for them, but in the end they did not want to try it.
exercises.explanation: Track the same direct object across both clauses.

exercises.prompt: I was going to lend you my notes, but someone else asked me for them first.
exercises.answer: Iba a prestarte mis apuntes, pero otra persona me los pidio primero.
exercises.answer_alt: Te iba a prestar mis apuntes, pero otra persona me los pidio primero.
exercises.explanation: The second clause uses both an indirect and a direct object pronoun.

exercises.prompt: Si la directora te da una oportunidad, aprovechala y demuestrale que puede confiar en ti.
exercises.answer: If the director gives you an opportunity, take advantage of it and show her that she can trust you.
exercises.answer_alt: If the director gives you an opportunity, make the most of it and show her that she can trust you.
exercises.explanation: This review sentence includes a direct object command and an indirect object in the next clause.

exercises.prompt: They brought us the desserts, but they served them to the other table first by mistake.
exercises.answer: Nos trajeron los postres, pero por error se los sirvieron primero a la otra mesa.
exercises.explanation: Use 'se los' in the second clause.

exercises.prompt: No queria interrumpirla, pero tenia que hacerle una pregunta importante.
exercises.answer: I did not want to interrupt her, but I had to ask her an important question.
exercises.explanation: This sentence contrasts direct and indirect object use naturally.

exercises.prompt: My aunt recommended the book to me, and now I am recommending it to you.
exercises.answer: Mi tia me recomendo el libro y ahora yo te lo recomiendo.
exercises.explanation: A clean review sentence with 'te lo'.

exercises.prompt: Cuando termines el informe, enviamelo y yo se lo paso al cliente.
exercises.answer: When you finish the report, send it to me and I will pass it on to the client.
exercises.answer_alt: When you finish the report, send it to me and I will send it to the client.
exercises.explanation: This sentence chains two double-object actions together.
